steps.prompt: Use the distill skill to extract an initial Allium specification from the JavaScript source files in this directory (models.js, service.js, routes.js, server.js). Write the result to spec.allium. Aim for a reasonable first pass: the Todo entity with its status enum and field set, the main business rules (createTodo, completeTodo, archiveTodo, reviveTodo, expireOverdue), an HTTP surface from routes.js, and a config block for REVIVE_GRACE_MS. It's acceptable if some details are missed — subsequent weed passes will fill them in. Validate with `allium check spec.allium` before finishing.

Use the weed skill in update-spec mode against spec.allium and the JavaScript source files in this directory. Walk every rule's requires and ensures clauses, every status transition the code performs, and every error code in service.js. Find anything the spec does not yet capture and add it. Do not remove anything. Validate with `allium check spec.allium` before finishing.

Run weed in update-spec mode against spec.allium again, focusing on process-level gaps: declared transitions in the spec that no rule produces (and vice versa), surface-trigger coverage (every external-stimulus rule should have a matching surface entry point), undeclared transitions in the code, and invariant enforcement. Add any missing constructs to the spec; do not remove existing ones. Validate before finishing.

Final weed pass in update-spec mode against spec.allium. This time look for: invariants the code enforces (e.g. RuleViolation thresholds, status preconditions, time-window guards), config values that should be hoisted into a config block (REVIVE_GRACE_MS and any other constants), and any external actors or integration boundaries not yet declared. Validate before finishing.

Use the tend skill to polish spec.allium. Ensure section ordering matches the language reference (use, given, external entities, value types, contracts, enumerations, entities, config, defaults, rules, invariants, actors, surfaces). Extract repeated inline enums into named enums where the same set of values appears on multiple fields. Tighten rule clauses where the weed passes added redundant guards. Make the spec read coherently end-to-end. Do not change any rule's behaviour; refactor for clarity only. Validate with `allium check spec.allium` before finishing.
